why: Artificial becomes fake adjacent in feigned and not genuine uses but remains broader and often neutral
main_evidence: WordReference artificial plus Merriam-Webster fake plus artificial tears and manners

why: False overlaps on deceptive and substitute uses but is broader because it also targets statements beliefs and loyalties
Counterfeit requires imitation with intent to deceive and is therefore sharper and narrower than artificial
Sham aligns with pretence and feigned display which matches only one branch of artificial
main_risk: Tends to be social or performative

why: Synthetic shares the non natural and industrial material branch strongly but is more chemically specific
main_evidence: WordReference synthetic plus regulatory and additive evidence

why: Imitation shares substitute and copy logic with some artificial objects but presupposes a model being copied
main_evidence: WordReference imitation and artificial flowers evidence
main_risk: Not all artificial things imitate originals

why: Simulated shares appearance and modeling logic and often sits between artificial and realistic
Realistic can describe the quality of an artificial object without making it real
main_evidence: Realistic artificial skin and realistic artificial flowers
main_risk: Could be misread as closeness to real rather than to artificial

why: Real directly opposes counterfeit imitation and artificial in authenticity uses
main_risk: Real has very broad philosophical senses

why: Genuine opposes artificial especially in sincerity and provenance contexts
Authentic opposes false or imitation and adds authority or verified origin
Natural is the strongest origin contrast to artificial and a major consumer label opposite
main_risk: Not every natural use equals genuine

why: Constructed captures the neutral built branch at the center of artificial
Manufactured captures the industrial production branch and often substitutes for artificial in non natural product contexts
main_risk: Figurative made up use can drift toward fake

why: Processed is less close than synthetic or manufactured but sits in the consumer and industrial orbit around artificial
main_evidence: Processed entry plus food labeling evidence
main_risk: Consumer discourse can smuggle in value judgments
